tasks.title: SQLite data-fetching layer — query all rows and return as list of dicts
tasks.description: Write a db.py helper (or extend the existing one) with a get_all_records() function that queries the SQLite database and returns a list of dicts keyed by column name. This is the data layer that all export formats will consume. Covers: test_export_json_structure, test_export_csv_header_row.

tasks.title: JSON export formatter — serialize list-of-dicts to JSON string/file
tasks.description: Write an export_json(records, output_path=None) function that serializes records to JSON. If output_path is None, write to stdout. If output_path is provided, write to file. Raise a clear error on unwritable paths. Covers: test_export_json_stdout, test_export_json_to_file, test_export_unwritable_path_exits_nonzero.

tasks.title: CSV export formatter — serialize list-of-dicts to CSV string/file
tasks.description: Write an export_csv(records, output_path=None) function using the stdlib csv module. Header row must match dict keys. If output_path is None, write to stdout. Covers: test_export_csv_stdout, test_export_csv_to_file, test_export_csv_header_row.

tasks.title: Click export command — wire --format and -o flags, empty-data guard
tasks.description: Register a @cli.command('export') Click command with --format (csv|json, default json) and -o/--output (file path, default None). Call get_all_records(); if empty, sys.exit(1) with message. Dispatch to export_json or export_csv. Covers: test_export_empty_data_exits_nonzero and integration of all other tests through the CLI entry point.
